outcome.headline: Agent submitted solution after a toy smoke test; scan_sequence returns -1 (test expects None) and double-applies load_motif_from_df to an already-normalized ndarray, causing 76.4 test failures.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: Runs sub-step assertions for 76.1, 76.2, 76.4 against h5 targets; 76.4 includes `assert scan_sequence(sequence, load_motif_from_df(data3),0.8) == None` for a uniform PWM, and passes the already-normalized matrix (ndarray) as the `matrix` argument.
outcome.what_agent_produced: solution.py with load_motif_from_df, compute_kld, pre-written generate_dna, and scan_sequence that re-invokes load_motif_from_df on its `matrix` arg and returns -1 instead of None when no positions exceed threshold.

failure_modes.name: Misread scan_sequence input type — treats already-loaded PWM matrix as a dict
failure_modes.description: The verifier passes the post-normalization numpy array (output of load_motif_from_df) as `matrix`, but the agent calls load_motif_from_df on it again, doing `data['A']` on a numpy array which raises an error and fails every non-trivial test case

counterfactual.X: call `load_motif_from_df(matrix)` inside scan_sequence
counterfactual.Y: use `matrix` directly as the already-normalized PWM (e.g., `pwm = np.asarray(matrix)`)

failure_modes.name: Double-normalization: scan_sequence re-calls load_motif_from_df on an already-normalized ndarray
failure_modes.description: scan_sequence unconditionally invokes pwm = load_motif_from_df(matrix); the verifier passes load_motif_from_df(data) (a numpy ndarray) as `matrix`, so accessing data['A'] on an ndarray raises TypeError/IndexError, failing every 76.4 case.
failure_modes.evidence_quote:
pwm = load_motif_from_df(matrix)
    kld = compute_kld(pwm)
    threshold = scale * kld

counterfactual.X: pwm = load_motif_from_df(matrix)
counterfactual.Y: pwm = matrix if isinstance(matrix, np.ndarray) else load_motif_from_df(matrix)

failure_modes.name: Misread of scan_sequence's `matrix` argument contract
failure_modes.description: The verifier invokes scan_sequence(sequence, load_motif_from_df(data), 0.8) — the matrix arg is already the normalized ndarray. The agent implemented scan_sequence to treat matrix as a raw dict PWM and re-invoked load_motif_from_df on it; additionally returned -1 instead of None when no window exceeds threshold.

failure_modes.name: Skipped official verifier tests

failure_modes.name: PWM normalization mismatch
failure_modes.description: load_motif_from_df L1-normalizes each position row after adding 1; reproduced evaluation fails all 76.1/76.2 HDF5 checks (e.g., uniform PWM target rows are all 0.5 with row sum 2.0, not unit-sum probabilities).

counterfactual.X: return -1
counterfactual.Y: return None

counterfactual.X: return -1
counterfactual.Y: return None

step 4 spec says scan_sequence accepts PWM dict ('matrix (PWM)') but one verifier case passes the already-normalized ndarray as the matrix arg, requiring scan_sequence to accept both types

outcome.headline: The agent wrote a plausible implementation from the prose spec, but did not run the benchmark verifier and submitted code that failed all evaluated local steps.

outcome.what_agent_produced: A solution that L1-normalizes PWM rows, computes KLD on that matrix, includes the prewritten generate_dna, and implements scan_sequence by re-calling load_motif_from_df on its matrix argument and returning -1 when no positions are found.

issues: Verifier target normalization conflicts with the written requirement: instruction.md says L1 normalization, while HDF5 targets are L2-normalized rows.

outcome.headline: Agent implemented the written L1-normalized solution, but the verifier expected different normalization and additional scan edge behavior; the agent only ran ad hoc checks and shipped a verifier-failing solution.

The targets require L2-like normalized matrices, exact KLD values, exact detected positions, and None for no detection.
outcome.what_agent_produced: The agent wrote /app/solution.py with all four functions. load_motif_from_df adds 1 and L1-normalizes each row; compute_kld sums p*log(p/0.25); generate_dna matches the pre-written code; scan_sequence calls load_motif_from_df(matrix), scans deterministically num_runs times, returns Counter.most_common(1)[0][0], and returns -1 when no positions exceed threshold.

issues: Verifier contradicts instruction.md: the task says L1 normalize rows after adding 1, while verifier targets are L2-normalized rows.

outcome.headline: The agent wrote a plausible l1-based implementation and an exact prewritten generator, but did not run the official verifier; the submitted code failed the verifier, including because scan_sequence expected a raw PWM dict while the tests pass an already-normalized matrix.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: test.sh runs test_outputs.py, skips prewritten step 76.3, and evaluates 76.1, 76.2, and 76.4 against HDF5 targets. The exposed targets expect L2-normalized motif rows, natural-log KLD on those rows, scan positions 163/654, and None for no detection.
outcome.what_agent_produced: A /app/solution.py with l1 row normalization, KLD against uniform background, the prewritten generate_dna body, and scan_sequence that calls load_motif_from_df(matrix), repeats deterministic scans, returns Counter.most_common(1)[0][0], and returns -1 when no position is detected.

issues: Verifier targets contradict the instruction to l1 normalize rows into probability distributions; they match L2 normalization instead.

task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: The local verifier exposes fixed test cases in problem_data.json and target outputs in test_data.h5, so a solution could hard-code those cases for the local tests.

outcome.headline: The agent implemented all required functions, but the verifier expected normalization inconsistent with the written task and the agent also shipped an insufficiently validated scanner.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: The verifier ran test_outputs.py over non-prewritten steps 76.1, 76.2, and 76.4; it compared load_motif_from_df and compute_kld against HDF5 targets and called scan_sequence(sequence, load_motif_from_df(data), 0.8), with uniform cases expecting None.
outcome.what_agent_produced: The agent produced /app/solution.py with L1 row normalization, KLD computation, the prewritten generate_dna, and scan_sequence that re-called load_motif_from_df on its matrix argument and returned -1 when no positions were detected. It only ran a custom smoke test.

failure_modes.name: Insufficient validation against representative call shape
failure_modes.description: The agent ran only a hand-written smoke test that passed the original PWM dict into scan_sequence, so it missed the verifier's call pattern where scan_sequence receives an already-normalized matrix. It then concluded the implementation was verified.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Run a quick Python test to verify the functions work correctly - test load_motif_from_df, compute_kld, and scan_sequence with sample data.

counterfactual.X: Run a representative test that calls scan_sequence(sequence, load_motif_from_df(data), 0.8) and checks the no-detection case.
counterfactual.Y: The type mismatch and -1-vs-None behavior would have been exposed before submission.

failure_modes.name: Scanner mishandled normalized matrix input
failure_modes.description: scan_sequence treated its matrix argument as a raw PWM dict and called load_motif_from_df(matrix). The task tests pass an already-normalized matrix into scan_sequence, so this implementation cannot handle the verifier's input type.

counterfactual.X: Accept both dict PWM inputs and already-normalized numpy arrays, and return None rather than -1 when no position is detected.
counterfactual.Y: The scanner would match the verifier's call shape and no-hit expectation.

reward_hacking.evidence: No evidence of hardcoded targets, test tampering, or attempts to read verifier files; the agent created /app/solution.py and ran a local smoke test.

issues: Verifier targets contradict the instruction's explicit L1 normalization requirement by expecting L2-normalized rows.
No ./trial/test_stdout.txt was mounted, so the exact verifier stdout requested by the audit prompt is unavailable.
The verifier assets include test cases and HDF5 targets under ./task/tests, making the scoring structure inspectable in this audit mount.
task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: test_outputs.py loads expected targets from test_data.h5; if those files are visible to a submitted solution, a malicious solution could read or overfit to them.

outcome.headline: scan_sequence calls load_motif_from_df on an already-normalized matrix passed by the verifier, crashing step 76.4 and yielding zero reward despite plausible load_motif/compute_kld code.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: test_outputs.py runs HDF5-backed assertions for steps 76.1, 76.2, and 76.4 (76.3 pre-written/skipped); step 76.4 calls scan_sequence(sequence, load_motif_from_df(data), 0.8) and expects the insertion index or None for uniform PWM (tests/test_outputs.py:67-137, problem_data.json:31-37).
outcome.what_agent_produced: /app/solution.py with load_motif_from_df (+1 then row L1-normalize), compute_kld (uniform background), exact generate_dna stub, and scan_sequence that re-invokes load_motif_from_df(matrix), uses a redundant num_runs loop, and returns -1 when nothing exceeds threshold.

failure_modes.name: Re-normalized scan matrix input
failure_modes.description: The verifier passes a normalized numpy PWM from load_motif_from_df into scan_sequence, but the agent treats matrix as a raw dict and calls load_motif_from_df(matrix) again, which raises IndexError on ndarray indexing and prevents step 76.4 from running.

counterfactual.X: Treat matrix as the pre-normalized PWM array (use it directly for log-odds and compute_kld(matrix)), and return None when no position passes the threshold.
counterfactual.Y: Called load_motif_from_df(matrix) on the verifier-supplied ndarray and returned -1 instead of None.

failure_modes.name: Skipped official verifier tests
failure_modes.description: After writing solution.py, the agent ran only a hand-written smoke test on a toy PWM and declared success without executing the SciCode harness that uses HDF5 targets and the generate_dna/scan_sequence integration cases.

issues: instruction.md labels scan_sequence's matrix parameter as PWM dict, while problem_data.json tests always pass load_motif_from_df(data) (normalized ndarray), which invites API misunderstanding.
task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Grading compares outputs to hidden HDF5 targets via subprocess-isolated per-step scripts; passing without correct motif scanning requires matching secret expected values.

outcome.headline: The agent wrote all four functions but failed every graded sub-step because PWM normalization did not match HDF5 targets and scan_sequence re-called load_motif_from_df on an already-normalized ndarray, then submitted without running the official verifier.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: test_outputs.py (invoked by tests/test.sh) runs HDF5-backed assertions for steps 76.1, 76.2, and 76.4 (76.3 pre-written/skipped); each step passes only if all its test cases exit 0, then sets reward = steps_passed / total_evaluated (3 steps).
outcome.what_agent_produced: /app/solution.py with load_motif_from_df (+1 then L1 row-normalize to sum 1), compute_kld, exact generate_dna stub, and scan_sequence that re-normalizes via load_motif_from_df(matrix) and returns -1 when no hits.

failure_modes.name: PWM normalization mismatch
failure_modes.description: load_motif_from_df adds 1 and L1-normalizes each row to sum 1.0, but HDF5 targets for 76.1 are (raw+1) scaled by per-row divisors so rows sum to values like 1.723 or 2.0 (uniform case), causing all 76.1 and 76.2 np.allclose assertions to fail.

failure_modes.name: Skipped verifier, scan API bug
failure_modes.description: After a trivial python3 -c smoke test on toy data, the agent marked the task complete without running /tests/test_outputs.py. Its scan_sequence calls load_motif_from_df(matrix) even though tests pass an ndarray from load_motif_from_df(data), causing IndexError on 76.4, and returns -1 instead of None for no-detection cases.

counterfactual.X: Use the matrix argument directly in scan_sequence (no second load_motif_from_df) and return None when no positions exceed threshold, then run python3 /tests/test_outputs.py.
counterfactual.Y: Re-invoked load_motif_from_df on the ndarray, returned -1 on empty results, and submitted after only checking a hand-written toy example.

issues: HDF5 76.1 targets contradict instruction.md: rows labeled probability distributions sum to 2.0 for uniform PWM, not 1.0
problem_data.json 76.4 test cases 4–5 reference undefined variable data (should be data2)
trial bundle lacks test_stdout.txt
task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Verifier compares against fixed HDF5 targets; passing requires matching the reference numerical convention, not just syntax or import checks.

outcome.headline: The agent shipped solution.py with incorrect PWM handling and scan_sequence re-normalizing an ndarray, then marked complete after a toy self-test while the SciCode verifier scored 0/3 substeps.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: test.sh runs test_outputs.py, which executes each sub-step in problem_data.json against /app/solution.py with HDF5 targets from test_data.h5 (76.1 load_motif_from_df, 76.2 compute_kld, 76.4 scan_sequence+generate_dna; 76.3 skipped as pre-written); reward is steps_passed/total_steps (test_outputs.py:20-44,67-137).
outcome.what_agent_produced: /app/solution.py with load_motif_from_df (+1 then per-position L1 row normalize), compute_kld (sum of p*log(p/0.25)), provided generate_dna, and scan_sequence that calls load_motif_from_df(matrix), thresholds log-odds at scale*KLD, returns Counter mode or -1.

failure_modes.name: Re-applies PWM loader on array
failure_modes.description: scan_sequence always calls load_motif_from_df(matrix), but the verifier passes an already-built numpy PWM from load_motif_from_df(data); this raises IndexError on data['A'] and prevents 76.4 from running.

counterfactual.X: Treat matrix as the pre-normalized PWM array when it is already an ndarray (pwm = matrix).
counterfactual.Y: Called load_motif_from_df(matrix) even when matrix was already the output of load_motif_from_df.

failure_modes.name: Toy test only before submit
failure_modes.description: After writing solution.py, the agent ran a hand-written python3 -c on a tiny PWM and declared success; it never ran /tests/test_outputs.py or the HDF5-backed assertions.

failure_modes.name: PWM normalization mismatch
failure_modes.description: load_motif_from_df L1-normalizes each position row after adding 1; reproduced evaluation fails all 76.1/76.2 HDF5 checks (e.g., uniform PWM target rows are all 0.5 with row sum 2.0, not unit-sum probabilities).
failure_modes.evidence_quote: # L1 normalize each row (so each row sums to 1)

issues: instruction.md says each PWM row becomes an L1 probability distribution, but 76.1 gold for uniform PWM is [0.5,0.5,0.5,0.5] per row (row sum 2.0).
76.4 tests pass load_motif_from_df(data) ndarray into scan_sequence while Step 4 text says matrix (PWM) dict—API contract is easy to misread.
task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Scoring compares function outputs to HDF5 reference targets per sub-step; passing requires matching the gold numerical pipeline, not just file presence.

outcome.headline: The agent submitted after a trivial smoke test without running the official verifier; scan_sequence crashes on the test API and all three graded sub-steps failed (reward 0).
outcome.what_verifier_checked: test.sh runs python3 /tests/test_outputs.py, which executes HDF5-backed assertions from problem_data.json for steps 76.1, 76.2, and 76.4 (76.3 skipped as pre-written); reward is 1.0 only if all evaluated steps pass (test_outputs.py:20-44, 67-137).
outcome.what_agent_produced: /app/solution.py implementing load_motif_from_df (+1 then row L1 normalize), compute_kld, the provided generate_dna, and scan_sequence that calls load_motif_from_df(matrix) again, loops num_runs over a deterministic sliding-window scan, and returns -1 when no position exceeds threshold.

failure_modes.name: Re-normalizes ndarray in scan
failure_modes.description: In step 2 the agent wrote scan_sequence to call load_motif_from_df(matrix) even though the verifier passes an already-normalized numpy PWM from load_motif_from_df(data). That re-entry treats the ndarray like a dict and raises IndexError, so step 76.4 fails before any binding-position check.

counterfactual.X: Use the matrix argument directly as the PWM (it is already load_motif_from_df output in tests) and return None when no position passes threshold
counterfactual.Y: Called pwm = load_motif_from_df(matrix) on the pre-normalized ndarray and returned -1 instead of None

failure_modes.name: Skipped official verifier
failure_modes.description: After writing solution.py in step 3 the agent ran only a hand-written python3 -c smoke test on a toy PWM and declared success in steps 4–5. It never executed /tests/test_outputs.py, so HDF5 assertion failures on 76.1–76.4 and the scan_sequence API crash were not discovered before submission.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Run a quick Python test to verify the functions work correctly - test load_motif_from_df, compute_kld, and scan_sequence with sample data.

issues: problem_data.json step 76.4 test cases 5–6 call load_motif_from_df(data) but only define data2/data3 in that scope, causing NameError even for a correct scanner

HDF5 targets for 76.1/76.2 do not match row-wise L1 probability vectors produced by the instruction’s stated +1-then-normalize recipe (e.g., uniform PWM rows sum to 2.0 in targets vs 1.0 for literal row L1 norm)
task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Scoring compares function outputs to HDF5 reference values per sub-step; passing requires numerically matching hidden targets across diverse PWM fixtures, not a single stdout pattern.

outcome.headline: The agent shipped PWM/scanner code that fails all three graded SciCode steps: motif normalization does not match HDF5 targets, and scan_sequence crashes when given the pre-normalized matrix the tests pass.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: test_outputs.py evaluates steps 76.1, 76.2, and 76.4 (76.3 pre-written) by embedding /app/solution.py into temp scripts and asserting outputs against HDF5 targets via np.allclose or None checks; reward = steps_passed / total_evaluated (test_outputs.py:20-44, 67-137).
outcome.what_agent_produced: /app/solution.py with load_motif_from_df (add 1, row L1-normalize), compute_kld (global sum over matrix), the prescribed generate_dna, and scan_sequence that re-calls load_motif_from_df(matrix), thresholds log-odds with scale*KLD, returns Counter mode or -1.

failure_modes.name: Toy test false pass
failure_modes.description: After writing solution.py in step 2, the agent ran only a hand-written smoke test on a tiny PWM and declared success in steps 4–5 without ever running the official SciCode harness, so normalization and scan_sequence API bugs never surfaced.

counterfactual.X: Run the task verifier (python3 /tests/test_outputs.py) before marking complete.
counterfactual.Y: Ran a custom python3 -c smoke test on toy data and treated its output as sufficient proof.

failure_modes.name: Double-normalized scan matrix
failure_modes.description: scan_sequence always calls load_motif_from_df(matrix), but step 76.4 tests pass an already-normalized numpy array from load_motif_from_df(data), causing IndexError on data['A'] and guaranteeing step 76.4 failure even if earlier steps were fixed.

issues: instruction.md says each PWM row becomes a probability distribution after L1 normalization, but HDF5 targets scale rows above unit sum (e.g., uniform rows sum to 2.0 with entries 0.5).
Step 76.4 tests pass load_motif_from_df(data) into scan_sequence while the prose still labels matrix as PWM dict, which invites re-normalizing inside scan_sequence.
task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Ground-truth values live in test_data.h5; the agent cannot pass without matching hidden numeric targets.

aggregated_from.note: Failure modes: union of all judge×round audits, deduped by (A,B,C,D). Outcome/verdicts: majority vote (step_where_lost = median). Individual passes are kept under `passes`. Step indices remapped from the audits' 1-indexing to the viewer's 0-indexing.
